NNFR.plain: Case was called but not heard today. No progress was made.

S/O.plain: Case was postponed to be taken up later during the same day or session.

HEARD.plain: The case was actually heard. Arguments or evidence may have been presented.

ADJOURNED.plain: Case was postponed to a future date. No arguments were heard today.

ADJ.plain: Case was postponed to a future date. No arguments were heard today.

DISMISSED.plain: The case or application was dismissed by the court.

ALLOWED.plain: The court allowed the application or petition filed.

DISPOSED.plain: The case has been concluded and disposed of by the court.

PART HEARD.plain: Arguments were started but not completed. Will continue on the next date.

RESERVED.plain: Arguments are completed. The judge has reserved the order/judgment for a later date.

EXPARTE.plain: The case was heard in the absence of one party. This is significant — the absent party may lose by default.

UNCONTESTED.plain: No one opposed the matter. It was granted without argument.

WITHDRAWN.plain: The case or application was withdrawn by the party who filed it.

SETTLED.plain: The parties reached an agreement. The case may be closed.

MEDIATION.plain: The court has sent this case for mediation — both parties will try to reach a compromise outside court.

COMPLAINCE.plain: The court is checking if its earlier order has been followed by the parties.

COMPLIANCE.plain: The court is checking if its earlier order has been followed by the parties.

FOR ORDERS.plain: The judge will pass an order today or on the next date. Arguments are already done.

FOR JUDGMENT.plain: The final judgment will be pronounced. This is a critical date.

FOR EVIDENCE.plain: Documents or witness testimony will be presented today.

FOR ARGUMENTS.plain: Lawyers will present oral arguments before the judge.

FOR CROSS EXAMINATION.plain: The opposing lawyer will question your witnesses.

FOR CHIEF EXAMINATION.plain: Your lawyer will question your own witnesses to present your case.

NOTICE.plain: The court has issued a notice to the opposite party to appear and respond.

SUMMONS.plain: The court has issued a summons — a formal order requiring someone to appear in court.

WARRANT.plain: The court has issued a warrant. This is a serious enforcement order.

STAY.plain: The court has ordered a pause on some action or order until further notice.

INTERIM ORDER.plain: A temporary order has been passed by the court while the main case continues.

SINE DIE.plain: The case has been postponed with NO next date fixed. It will only resume when someone applies to reschedule.

TRANSFERRED.plain: The case has been moved to a different court or judge.

CLUBBED.plain: This case has been merged/combined with another similar case for joint hearing.

RESTORATION.plain: A previously dismissed or closed case is being revived/restored.

FIL.plain: The case has been filed. Documents are being checked by the court registry.

ADM.plain: The court is deciding whether to officially accept and hear this case.

NOTICE.plain: The court has sent a formal notice to the other side asking them to respond.

WS.plain: The opposite party is filing their written response to your case.

FRA.plain: The judge is identifying the key questions (issues) that need to be decided in this case.

PE.plain: Your side is presenting documents and witnesses to support your case.

DE.plain: The other side is presenting their documents and witnesses.

ARG.plain: Both lawyers give their final oral arguments summarizing the entire case.

JUD.plain: The judge will announce the final decision. This is the last stage.

EXECUTION.plain: The judgment has been passed. This stage is about enforcing the court's decision.

stages.name: Admission
stages.description: Court decides whether to accept the case

stages.name: Framing of Issues
stages.description: Judge identifies key questions to decide

stages.name: Plaintiff's Evidence
stages.description: Your side presents documents and witnesses

stages.name: Defendant's Evidence
stages.description: Other side presents documents and witnesses
